name: Insecure Inherited Permissions
description: A product defines insecure permissions for a parent object, and objects created within it automatically inherit those overly permissive settings. This can allow unintended access or modification of sensitive child resources.
detection_pattern: Look for STRUCTURAL nodes (parent directories, container objects, namespaces) with permissive AUTH settings that propagate to child STORAGE nodes (created files, subdirectories, child objects) without a CONTROL mediator (explicit child permission override, inheritance restriction, permission mask) that ensures child objects receive appropriately restrictive permissions independent of the parent.
